title: Support non-parameterized mailbox
body:
**Self-contained example**

Example from https://www.chipverify.com/systemverilog/systemverilog-mailbox:

```sv
module mailbox_tb;
	// Create a new mailbox that can hold utmost 2 items
  	mailbox 	mbx = new(2);

  	// Block1: This block keeps putting items into the mailbox
  	// The rate of items being put into the mailbox is 1 every ns
  	initial begin
		for (int i=0; i < 5; i++) begin
        	#1 mbx.put (i);
        	$display ("[%0t] Thread0: Put item #%0d, size=%0d", $time, i, mbx.num());
      	end
    end

  	// Block2: This block keeps getting items from the mailbox
  	// The rate of items received from the mailbox is 2 every ns
	initial begin
		forever begin
			int idx;
			#2 mbx.get (idx);
          	$display ("[%0t] Thread1: Got item #%0d, size=%0d", $time, idx, mbx.num());
		end
	end
endmodule
```

**What output from that test indicates it is wrong, and what is the correct or expected output?  (Or, please make test self-checking if possible.)**
The simulation runs w.o. warnings and errors with VCS, Vivado and Questa SIM.

Verilator compile log:

```
%Error: mailbox_tb.sv:3:4: Class parameter type without default value is never given value (IEEE 1800-2023 6.20.1): 'T'
                         : ... note: In instance 'mailbox_tb'
    3 |    mailbox  mbx = new(2);
      |    ^~~~~~~
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.045 for more assistance.
%Error: /path/to/verilator/include/verilated_std.sv:35:12: Parameter type without default value is never given value (IEEE 1800-2023 6.20.1): 'T'
   35 |       type T
      |            ^
        /path/to/verilator/include/verilated_std.sv:2:1: ... note: In file included from 'verilated_std.sv'
%Error: Exiting due to 2 error(s)

```

**What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?**
```
verilator  --binary  mailbox_tb.sv  --top mailbox_tb -Wno-lint  -CFLAGS -std=c++20
```


**What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?**
Verilator 5.045 devel rev v5.044-92-g7c6c6a684

VCS output:
```

           V C S   S i m u l a t i o n   R e p o r t

```

**What OS and distribution are you using?**
Rocky Linux 8.10
